description_long: This house features a simple rectangular structure with a prominent gable roof covered in overlapping shingles, displaying a mix of muted colors. The exterior walls are constructed from uniformly laid gray bricks, giving the building a solid and traditional appearance. The roof is supported by a visible internal framework of wooden trusses, painted in alternating blue and pink hues, which can be seen through the semi-transparent roofing material. There are no visible windows or doors on the exterior, suggesting a minimalistic or unfinished design. The roof extends slightly beyond the walls, providing a modest overhang for weather protection. The overall form and materials evoke a utilitarian, suburban residential style.
description: A rectangular brick house with a gable roof, visible wooden trusses, and no apparent windows or doors. The roof features overlapping shingles in muted colors and a slight overhang.
description_view_0: End view of the house showing the gable roof peak and the brick wall with no visible openings.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the full length of the house, the semi-transparent roof, and the colorful internal truss structure.
description_view_2: Opposite end view, mirroring view 0, with the gable roof and plain brick wall.
description_view_3: Side view similar to view 1, showing the internal trusses and the length of the house.
attribute_entryway_design: no visible entryway; plain brick walls with no doors or decorative elements
